title: Paris, Lucerne, Venice, Florence & Rome.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Scheduled trains linking five classic cities, with private transfers at your arrival and departure points.

transport.1: Paris airport to Paris hotel private transfer.
transport.2: Paris City Tour with Seine cruise and Eiffel Tower 2nd floor, 4 hrs coach tour.
transport.3: Paris to Lucerne train ticket, II Class — start of a 4-day consecutive Swiss Travel Pass.
transport.4: Mount Titlis excursion using the Swiss Travel Pass, with a Lake Lucerne cruise also free on the pass.
transport.6: Lucerne to Venice (Mestre) train, II Class, on the final day of Swiss Travel Pass validity.
transport.8: Venice (Mestre) to Florence train, II Class.
transport.9: Florence to Rome train, II Class.

inclusions: Paris airport to hotel private transfer on arrival
Paris City Tour, Seine Cruise and Eiffel Tower 2nd Floor, 4 hrs coach tour
Paris to Lucerne train ticket, II Class
Swiss Travel Pass, 4 consecutive days, II Class
Mount Titlis excursion and Lake Lucerne cruise, covered by Swiss Travel Pass
Onward train tickets, II Class, between Lucerne, Venice, Florence and Rome
10 nights accommodation across 5 cities with daily breakfast

private.name: Private Exclusive Coach
private.blurb: A private vehicle on disposal for most of the journey, with guided tours in Paris, Venice and Rome.

transport.1: Paris airport to Paris hotel private transfer.
transport.2: Private Paris city tour with English-speaking guide, 3 hrs, plus a 1-hour Seine river cruise.
transport.3: Private transfer Paris to Lucerne; vehicle now on disposal for 8 hrs daily through Day 11.
transport.4: Private excursion to Titlis and Engelberg.
transport.7: Venice walking tour, 2 hrs with English-speaking guide.
transport.10: Rome city tour with English-speaking guide, 3 hrs.

inclusions: Paris airport to hotel private transfer on arrival
Private vehicle on disposal for 8 hrs daily, Day 3 through Day 11
Private Paris city tour with English-speaking guide, 3 hrs
Private excursion to Titlis and Engelberg
Venice walking tour, 2 hrs with English-speaking guide
Rome city tour with English-speaking guide, 3 hrs
10 nights accommodation across 5 cities with daily breakfast

days.title: Arrive Paris
days.desc: Arrive into the City of Light and transfer to your hotel. Depending on arrival time, take a first stroll along the Seine or up to Montmartre and Sacré-Cœur for sweeping views over the rooftops.

days.title: Paris City Tour & Eiffel Tower
days.desc: A guided coach tour takes in the Champs-Élysées, Arc de Triomphe, Notre-Dame and the Louvre, followed by a Seine river cruise and a visit to the Eiffel Tower's 2nd floor for panoramic views across Paris.
days.shopping: The Champs-Élysées and the grand department stores of Boulevard Haussmann, Galeries Lafayette and Printemps, are Paris's classic shopping stretches.

days.title: Paris → Lucerne
days.desc: Board the train for Lucerne, arriving in this compact lakeside city framed by snow-capped peaks. Walk across the wooden Chapel Bridge, one of Europe's oldest covered bridges, and along the Reuss River in the old town.

days.title: Mount Titlis Excursion
days.desc: Ride the rotating Titlis Rotair cableway up to almost 3,000 metres for glacier views and, snow permitting, the Ice Flyer chairlift and Glacier Cave. Return to Lucerne with time for a cruise on Lake Lucerne.
days.taste: Swiss chocolate and a wheel of melted raclette or a pot of cheese fondue are the classic warming treats after a day on the mountain.

days.title: Lucerne at Leisure
days.desc: A free day to explore Lucerne at your own pace — the Lion Monument carved into the rock face, the Swiss Museum of Transport, or a further lake cruise to one of the lakeside villages.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure in Lucerne — optional excursions such as Mount Pilatus available.

days.title: Lucerne → Venice
days.desc: Travel south over the Alps into Italy, arriving at Mestre on the Venetian mainland, the gateway to Venice's historic islands just a short train or bus ride across the lagoon.

days.title: Venice
days.desc: Cross into Venice to wander St Mark's Square, the Doge's Palace and the Rialto Bridge, with canals and gondolas at every turn in one of the world's most singular cities.
days.experience: A gondola glide along the smaller canals away from the crowds is the classic Venetian experience, best taken in the early evening light.

days.title: Venice → Florence
days.desc: Continue on to Florence, cradle of the Renaissance. Spend the afternoon around the Duomo, Ponte Vecchio and Piazza della Signoria before dinner in the Oltrarno district.
days.taste: A Florentine bistecca alla fiorentina, a thick T-bone steak grilled rare, paired with a glass of Chianti Classico.

days.title: Florence → Rome
days.desc: Travel south to Rome, the Eternal City. Settle in with an evening walk past the Trevi Fountain and the Spanish Steps, both within easy reach of the historic centre.

days.title: Rome City Tour
days.desc: A guided city tour covers the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Vatican-area highlights, tracing over two thousand years of history from ancient Rome to the Renaissance and Baroque.
days.experience: Standing inside the Colosseum, where gladiators once fought before 50,000 spectators, remains one of Europe's most powerful historical experiences.

days.title: Depart Rome
days.desc: Free time until your transfer to the airport, depending on flight schedule — a last espresso in a Roman piazza is the perfect send-off.

about.title: Best season
about.body: May–September for warm weather across all five cities; the Alpine cable cars at Titlis operate year-round, snow-dependent for some features.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Paris and Lucerne are temperate with cooler Alpine air; Venice, Florence and Rome turn hot and humid in summer, so pack layers for the mountain leg.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Euro (€) in France and Italy, Swiss Franc (CHF) in Switzerland; all four countries lie within the Schengen area with no border checks between them.

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: Book the Eiffel Tower, Titlis cableway and Colosseum well ahead in summer — all three sell out days in advance at peak season.

goodToKnow.title: Rail travel
goodToKnow.body: Seat reservations are included on the international legs. The Swiss Travel Pass also covers local trams and some museum admissions in Lucerne.

goodToKnow.title: Your vehicle
goodToKnow.body: Driver working hours are limited by EU law, so day-trip timings are planned around rest breaks. Luggage space fits one large case per guest.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Warm layers and sturdy shoes for the Titlis excursion, plus lighter clothing for the Italian cities further south.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards are accepted almost everywhere; keep some local cash for markets and smaller cafés. A 10% tip in restaurants is customary, not obligatory.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
